items.passage: Wei Ming went ___ the hawker centre ___ he was hungry.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'to' shows direction; 'because' gives the reason.

items.passage: Siti stayed at home ___ she was sick, ___ she still finished her homework.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'because' gives the reason; 'but' shows a contrast.

items.passage: The cat sat ___ the sofa ___ waited for its food.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'on' tells where; 'and' joins the two actions.

items.passage: We waited ___ the bus stop ___ it started to rain.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'at' shows the place; 'when' shows the time something happened.

items.passage: Kavitha likes durians ___ her brother ___ not.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'but' shows the contrast; 'does' completes 'does not'.

items.passage: Put your shoes ___ the rack ___ come inside.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'on' tells where to put the shoes; 'and' joins the two instructions.

items.passage: ___ it was raining, the boys ___ played football at the field.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'Although' shows a contrast; 'still' means they played in spite of the rain.

items.passage: You may have an ice cream ___ a fruit, ___ not both.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'or' gives a choice; 'but' adds the limit that you cannot have both.

items.passage: The library is ___ the second floor, ___ the lift is out of order today.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'on' is used for floors; 'but' shows the contrast about the lift.

items.passage: Ravi woke up early ___ he could catch the first bus ___ school.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'so' shows the purpose; 'to' shows where the bus goes.

items.passage: Nurul put the plate ___ the table ___ went to wash her hands.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'on' tells where the plate went; 'and' joins the two actions.

items.passage: It was very hot, ___ we opened the windows ___ turned on the fan.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'so' shows the result of it being hot; 'and' joins the two actions.

items.passage: The keys are ___ the drawer ___ the top shelf.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'in' is used for inside the drawer; 'on' is used for the shelf.

items.passage: Ravi ran fast ___ he still missed the bus ___ waited for the next one.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'but' shows the contrast; 'and' joins what he did next.

items.passage: You can pay ___ cash ___ by card at the counter.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'in cash' is the set phrase; 'or' gives the choice between two ways.

items.passage: Siti sat ___ her friends ___ ate her lunch.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'with' shows who she sat beside; 'and' joins the two actions.

items.passage: ___ he was tired, Ahmad finished his homework ___ went to bed.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'Although' shows a contrast; 'then' shows what happened next.

items.passage: We can go to the beach ___ the park, ___ not both today.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'or' gives a choice; 'but' adds the limit that you cannot have both.

items.passage: Put the rubbish ___ the bin ___ wash your hands.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'in' tells where the rubbish goes; 'and' joins the two instructions.

items.passage: The shop was closed, ___ we came back the next day ___ bought the book.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'so' shows the result; 'and' joins what we did next.

items.passage: Kavitha waited ___ the MRT station ___ her brother arrived.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'at' shows the place; 'until' shows she waited up to the time he arrived.

items.passage: The cat jumped ___ the wall ___ ran away.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'over' tells how it crossed the wall; 'and' joins the two actions.

items.passage: I like both durian ___ mango, ___ I cannot finish them alone.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'and' joins the two fruits; 'but' shows the contrast.

items.passage: ___ it rained heavily, the match ___ went on as planned.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: 'Although' shows a contrast; 'still' means it went on in spite of the rain.

CL-grammar-cloze.iCanStatement: I can choose the right joining word for each blank.
CL-grammar-cloze.explanation: Read the whole sentence first. Little words like 'and', 'but', 'because', 'so', 'to', 'on' show how ideas link — reason, contrast, place or choice. Each word in the bank is used once, so match the easy ones first.

workedExamples.text: I ran because I was late.
It was hot but I felt fine.
workedExamples.note: 'but' shows a contrast between two ideas.
